class.name: SentryMetrics
class.description:
A dedicated API for emitting metrics to Sentry. Access this API through [member SentrySDK.metrics]. [SentryMetrics] provides methods for emitting different metric types: [method count] for incrementing counters, [method gauge] for tracking point-in-time values, and [method distribution] for recording value distributions. [b]Note:[/b] The [member SentryExperimental.enable_metrics] option in [member SentryOptions.experimental] must be enabled for metrics functionality to work. [codeblock] # Count occurrences SentrySDK.metrics.count("match_started") SentrySDK.metrics.count("in_app_purchase", 1, {"item": "sword_of_fire"})
# Track a point-in-time value SentrySDK.metrics.gauge("players_online", lobby.get_player_count())
# Record a value distribution SentrySDK.metrics.distribution("level_load_time", load_time, "millisecond") [/codeblock] Structured attributes support [code]bool[/code], [code]int[/code], [code]float[/code], and [code]String[/code] data types. Other types will be converted to strings.

method.name: count
method.description: Emits a counter metric. Counters track how many times something happened — each increment adds to a cumulative total. The [param value] defaults to [code]1[/code], making it convenient for counting occurrences. The optional [param attributes] dictionary adds structured metadata to the metric. [codeblock] SentrySDK.metrics.count("match_started") SentrySDK.metrics.count("enemies_spawned", wave_size, {"level": "castle"}) [/codeblock]

method.name: distribution
method.description: Emits a distribution metric. Distributions record numeric values to compute statistical aggregates such as p50, p95, avg, min, and max. The optional [param unit] specifies the unit of measurement. The optional [param attributes] dictionary adds structured metadata to the metric. [codeblock] SentrySDK.metrics.distribution("level_load_time", load_time, "millisecond") [/codeblock]

method.name: gauge
method.description: Emits a gauge metric. Gauges set a specific value at a point in time, like a snapshot. The optional [param unit] specifies the unit of measurement. The optional [param attributes] dictionary adds structured metadata to the metric. [codeblock] SentrySDK.metrics.gauge("players_online", lobby.get_player_count()) SentrySDK.metrics.gauge("memory_usage", OS.get_static_memory_usage(), "byte") [/codeblock]
